statement.p: A graph of a function f is given. Use the graph to write a formula for f in vertex form. You will need to identify the vertex and also one more point on the graph to find the leading coefficient a.

solution.p: A parabolas vertex form looks like f(x) = a(x-h)^2+k.
We can see the vertex is at (2,2), we have f(x) = a{\left(x-2\right)^{2}+2}.

Next, we can see the parabola passes the point (3,{-1}). (We could choose to use another point on the parabola.) We substitute this point (3,{-1}) into f(x) = a{\left(x-2\right)^{2}+2}, and solve for a:

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned}[t] f(x) \amp = a{\left(x-2\right)^{2}+2} \\ {-1} \amp = a(3-2)^2+2 \\ {-1} \amp = a(1)^2+2 \\ {-1} \amp = a+2 \\ {-3} \amp = a \\ a \amp = -3 \end{aligned} }
